svg.text: THAI
GRAMMAR & STRUCTURE
THAI WORD ORDER: SVO
Subject Verb Object (like English, unlike most Asian languages)
Chan gin khao. "I eat rice." (I eat rice)
Modifiers follow head: khao dam "rice black" = "black rice"
ISOLATING MORPHOLOGY
No inflection: no case, gender, number, tense marking on nouns/verbs
Tense: indicated by time words and aspect markers
cha (future) • kamlang (progressive) • laeo (perfect)
chan gin khao laeo "I have eaten rice" (laeo = already)
CLASSIFIERS
Classifiers: nouns need a classifier when counted
maew sāam tua "cat three CL:animal" = "three cats"
nangsuē sāam lem "book three CL:bound" = "three books"
Different classifier for every semantic category
POLITENESS PARTICLES
Kha (FEM speaker) / Khrap (MASC speaker): sentence-final respect
Sawatdii khrap (male: "hello") • Sawatdii kha (female: "hello")
KAREN GRAMMAR · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
KAREN WORD ORDER: SOV
Karen is SOV (Subject Object Verb) — opposite of Thai!
Postpositions (not prepositions): locative suffix after noun
Object before verb: more typical Tibeto-Burman pattern
KAREN MORPHOLOGY
Karen: also mostly isolating (like Thai)
Reduplication: common grammatical and semantic device
Verbs: can be serialized (verb-verb compounds)
KAREN CLASSIFIERS
Karen also uses classifiers when counting nouns!
Similar classifier categories to Thai (animate, flat, round...)
This areal feature shared across mainland SE Asia
GRAMMATICAL COMPARISON
Thai: SVO • Karen: SOV (different word orders!)
Both: isolating • both: classifiers • both: tonal
Thai: premodifiers • Karen: postpositions
PLATE 39c ALT · THAI / KAREN · GRAMMAR
